name: REX AMERICAN RESOURCES Corp
summary: REX American Resources Corporation is a Delaware holding company invested in three ethanol production entities: One Earth Energy, LLC (76.1% owned), NuGen Energy, LLC (99.7% owned), and Big River Resources, LLC (minority interest). The six facilities collectively shipped approximately 722 million gallons of ethanol in the twelve months ended January 31, 2026. REX produces ethanol via dry milling of corn, with co-products including distillers grains (DDGS) and distillers corn oil sold to animal feed, biodiesel, and chemical markets.

products.name: Denatured Ethanol
products.description: Renewable fuel produced via dry milling of corn, used as blend component, octane enhancer, and clean air additive in gasoline.

products.name: Dry Distillers Grains with Solubles (DDGS)
products.description: Principal by-product of ethanol dry milling process, sold as protein source in animal feed markets.

products.name: Distillers Corn Oil
products.description: Co-product from ethanol facilities sold to animal feed, biodiesel, and other chemical markets.

products.name: Modified/Wet Distillers Grains
products.description: Alternative by-product sold depending on market and operating conditions, with less liquid removed than DDGS.

partnerships.description: 76.1% majority-owned ethanol production entity located in Gibson City, IL.
99.7% majority-owned ethanol production entity located in Marion, SD.
Minority equity investment (5.7%-10.3%) in four ethanol production facilities across Iowa, Illinois, and Wisconsin.
